evals.prompt: I'm building a knowledge curation feature on our Open Social Drupal 10 site (os-knowledge-garden). We need a custom content entity type called KnowledgeResource for organizing external learning resources (articles, papers, tools, documentation) alongside our AI-indexed content. It should have base fields for: related_topic (entity_reference to node), author (entity_reference to user), resource_type (list_string with allowed values: article, research_paper, tool, documentation), and created timestamp. Include all necessary handlers - forms, list builder, access handler, and route provider. Put it in a module called knowledge_resource.
evals.expected_output: A complete content entity type with entity class, base fields, all handlers, and module scaffold that installs and creates entities successfully
expectations: Entity class has baseFieldDefinitions() that calls AND MERGES parent::baseFieldDefinitions($entity_type) (MUST call parent or lose id/uuid/langcode fields)
Base fields that should appear in forms use setDisplayOptions('form', [...]) (without this, fields won't appear in entity forms)
Base fields that should appear in listings use setDisplayOptions('view', [...]) (without this, fields won't render in entity view)
If entity has admin UI, entity annotation/attribute includes route_provider handler for auto-generated CRUD routes (NOT hand-writing all entity routes)
Entity handlers declaration includes 'form' handlers for add/edit/delete operations
This is a content entity per the prompt -- verify it does NOT incorrectly include config_export (config_export is for config entities only)
The module enables successfully: ddev drush en knowledge_resource returns exit code 0
Entity type installed: ddev drush php-eval confirms hasDefinition('knowledge_resource')
Entity can be created: ddev drush php-eval creates and saves entity without error
